name: Missouri
notes_normalized: Cannabis is legal in Missouri under state law for recreational adult use. Medical cannabis is also legal under a regulated state program in Missouri. Cannabis remains federally illegal in United States.
notes_raw: style="background:#196E92;" | {{Hs|1}} Cannabis in Missouri is legal for recreational use. In May 2014, Senate Bill 491 was enacted which reduced penalties for certain cannabis offenses. The bill also reduced penalties related to the sale and cultivation of cannabis, and eliminated the ban on probation or parole for third-time drug felony convictions. CBD oil legalized (2014). In July 2014, Governor Nixon signed into law House Bill 2238 – the Missouri Medical Marijuana Bill – to legalize the use of CBD oil to treat persistent seizures. The legislation "allows the Department of Agriculture to grow industrial hemp for research purposes and allows the use of hemp extract to treat certain individuals with epilepsy". HB 2238 only allows hemp extract that contains at least 5% cannabidiol (CBD) and no more than 0.3% tetrahydrocannabinol (THC). In February 2015, the state issued licenses to two non-profits to grow cannabis to produce the oil. In May 2014, Senate Bill 491 was enacted which reduced penalties for certain cannabis offenses. In particular, it eliminated the threat of jail time for first-time possession of up to 10 grams ( oz). The bill also reduced penalties related to the sale and cultivation of cannabis, and eliminated the ban on probation or parole for third-time drug felony convictions. The measure stipulated that possession of up to 35 grams ( oz) was to be processed in municipal court as a non-criminal offense, punishable by a maximum fine of $250. Cited persons would be processed in municipal court (instead of state court) and subject to a fine in the range of 100 to 500 dollars. Penalties were further reduced in February 2018 when the Board of Aldermen voted 24–0 to set a $25 fine for possession of up to 35 grams ( oz). The measure eliminated the threat of jail time and reduced the penalty to a $25 fine. The penalty was later eliminated in July 2020 by a 9–4 city council vote.

state_modifiers.federal_conflict: Cannabis remains federally illegal in United States.
